title: lithocarpus harmandii subsp. malacotrichus
intro: Lithocarpus harmandii subsp. malacotrichus, also known as the Chinese Bur oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, derived from its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides. The health benefits of Lithocarpus harmandii subsp. malacotrichus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve immune function. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its herbal preparations also include infusions, powders, and capsules, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
